Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants me to generate 20 open ended questions based on the provided pseudo BPMN diagram of a smartphone supply chain

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The questions need to be thought provoking and cover areas like process rationale improvements risk management decision making stakeholder communication and performance measurement

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: First I need to thoroughly understand the process as outlined Let me walk through each step

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: It starts with market research then RD for the prototype After that there is a parallel gateway splitting into securing components from different countries Then components are shipped to Malaysia for assembly Quality checks follow with a possible rework Then packaging shipping via air or sea customs distribution to regional centers local distribution tasks inventory monitoring and adjusting orders or proceeding

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Now for each section I can think of potential questions

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Why conduct market research before RD Maybe to validate product market fit

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: But the question could be deeper like how does the research influence design choices

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: In the component securing part the parallel gateways suggest simultaneous sourcing

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Questions could explore the rationale for multiple suppliers in different regions risks involved or how dependencies are managed if one supplier is delayed

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Shipping to Malaysia Why Malaysia specifically Cost regulations existing infrastructure

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: That is a good question Also potential bottlenecks in shipping all components there

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Assembly 24 7 Why round the clock To meet demand quickly What are the labor or maintenance implications

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Quality checks How is the pass fail criteria determined What is the cost of rework versus scrapping How does this impact overall efficiency

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Shipping methods Air vs cargo Questions about cost vs speed trade offs environmental impact or how demand dictates the choice

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Customs and compliance Different regions have different regulations How does the company ensure compliance across all markets What are the common challenges

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Distribution tasks The split into warehousing retailers ecommerce VIP Why these channels How are they balanced What is the role of real time data here

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Inventory monitoring How often is data reviewed What tools are used How do forecasts influence supplier orders

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Stakeholder communication Who needs to be informed at each stage How is transparency maintained especially with international partners

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Risk management Geopolitical issues supply chain disruptions Are there contingency plans How are risks assessed at each node

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Performance metrics What KPIs are tracked How do they measure success at each stage How is continuous improvement driven by data

Name: Validation - PE
Text: I need to ensure the questions are open ended avoiding yes no answers Also cover all the areas mentioned rationale improvements risks decision making communication performance

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Let me start drafting

Name: Validation - PE
Text: I need to make sure I have 20 questions

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Let me check So far I have 10 Lets think of more

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Component sourcing risks How are supplier dependencies managed to prevent bottlenecks if a primary supplier faces issues

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Rework process What initiates a rework versus a scrap decision and how is this process optimized to reduce waste

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Environmental impact How does the transportation strategy account for environmental sustainability goals alongside cost and speed considerations

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Regulatory compliance What measures are in place to ensure all products meet the regulatory standards of their destination markets

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Assembly challenges What are the primary challenges associated with 24 7 assembly operations and how are they mitigated

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Demand forecasting How are seasonal demand fluctuations and market trends incorporated into the inventory and production planning process

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Stakeholder coordination How is coordination maintained between RD procurement logistics and sales teams to ensure alignment with market needs

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Scalability How does the current supply chain model accommodate sudden spikes in demand or shifts in product specifications

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Data security What steps are taken to protect sensitive supply chain data shared with international partners and logistics providers

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: ROI on improvements How is the return on investment measured for process optimizations introduced at various stages of the supply chain

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Now compiling all these ensuring they are open ended and cover the required areas Let me count again Thats 20 Each question should prompt a detailed discussion exploring deeper aspects of the process without being leading or closed
